description_long: This is a weathered stone grave marker, likely a headstone, with a rounded top and a rectangular base. The front face features intricate carvings, including floral motifs and possibly a shield or crest, though erosion has softened many details. The upper edge is covered with a layer of moss or lichen, giving it a greenish tint and indicating long-term outdoor exposure. The back side is plainer, with faint, barely legible inscriptions that have faded over time. The stone sits on a slightly raised, rough base, suggesting it was set into the ground. The overall appearance is aged, with surface pitting and discoloration typical of old cemetery monuments.
description: A weathered, moss-covered stone headstone with ornate carvings on the front and faded inscriptions on the back.
description_view_0: The front view shows detailed carvings and decorative motifs, with moss along the top edge.
description_view_1: The left angled view highlights the thickness of the stone and the depth of the carvings, as well as the mossy top.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a plain, eroded surface with faint, nearly illegible inscriptions.
description_view_3: The right angled view shows the stone's thickness and the rough, mossy texture along the top and side.
